meta.title: Broken Chair – Geneva, Switzerland
meta.description: Complete travel guide to Broken Chair in Geneva.

intro.title: Discover Broken Chair
intro.description: Broken Chair (断椅) is a stunning water spring and natural phenomenon occurring near Muzinë in Vlorë County, Albania. A popular tourist attraction, the clear blue water of the river bubbles forth from a stunning, more than fifty-metre-deep pool. Divers have descended to fifty metres, but it is still unclear what the actual depth of the karst hole is.

visitGuide.title: Visit Tips
items: The water is extremely cold, usually around 10°C (50°F).
Swimming is officially prohibited to protect the natural environment.
There are cafes and restaurants nearby for refreshments.
The area is surrounded by lush green forests, perfect for a short hike.

sections.title: Location & Geography
sections.content: Located in southern Albania, Broken Chair is the initial water source of the Bistricë river, which ends in the Ionian Sea south of Saranda. The spring is situated in a dense forest of oaks and sycamores.

sections.title: Natural Phenomenon
sections.content: The spring owes its name to its striking appearance: the deep center is a dark blue, like the pupil of an eye, while the surrounding shallower water is a lighter shade of blue, like the iris.

items.question: Can you swim in Broken Chair?
items.answer: Swimming is officially prohibited to preserve the natural ecosystem, though some visitors still jump in. The water is also extremely cold (10°C).

items.question: Is there an entrance fee?
items.answer: Yes, there is a small entrance fee to access the nature reserve, and a parking fee if you bring a vehicle.

hours.title: Visiting Hours
hours.parkTime: Usually open from 7:00 AM to 7:00 PM (Summer hours may vary)
hours.bestTimeSpring: Spring: Lush greenery and fewer crowds
hours.bestTimeSummer: Summer: Great for cooling down, but very crowded
hours.bestTimeAutumn: Autumn: Beautiful foliage and pleasant weather
hours.bestTimeWinter: Winter: Quiet, but many facilities may be closed
hours.tip: Visit early in the morning to avoid large crowds and enjoy the peaceful atmosphere.

tickets.title: Admission Information
tickets.guidedPrice: It is recommended to book day tours in advance through local travel agencies in Saranda or Gjirokastër

transport.title: How to Get Here
transport.fromCenterDesc: Take Tram 15 and get off at the 'Nations' stop. It is about a 1-minute walk from there.
transport.fromStationDesc: Take Tram 15 (direction: Nations) from outside the station and get off at the terminus 'Nations'. The journey takes about 10-15 minutes.
transport.fromAirportDesc: Take Bus 5 or 28 and get off at the 'Nations' stop. The ride takes around 15-20 minutes.
transport.publicTransportDesc: Geneva's public transport (TPG) is highly convenient. Tourists staying in Geneva hotels receive a free 'Geneva Transport Card' for unlimited rides on local transport. You can reach Place des Nations via Tram 15, or Buses 5, 8, 11, 22, and 28.
transport.walkingDesc: If you are around the Right Bank of Lake Geneva or the International District, walking is a great option. The Broken Chair is prominently visible at the Place des Nations.
transport.drivingDesc: There is an underground parking facility nearby (Parking des Nations). Since it is located in the international organization district, street parking can be limited, so public transport is highly recommended.

route.title: Recommended Route
route.overview: Explore the natural beauty of the spring.
steps: Take a short hike in the surrounding forest

reviews.title: Visitor Reviews
reviews.declaration: Reviews can be viewed via Google Maps.
reviews.moreReviews: View more reviews on Google Maps

items.name: Sarah J.
items.text: Absolutely stunning natural beauty. The colors of the water are unbelievable!

items.name: Mark T.
items.text: Very beautiful, but gets quite crowded in the afternoon. Come early!

items.name: Elena R.
items.text: A magical place. The cold water is refreshing, and the surrounding forest is peaceful.

items.name: David L.
items.text: Great spot for photography. The walk from the parking lot is very pleasant.

items.name: Paula W.
items.text: Broken Chair was stunning. Definitely go to see it if you have the chance.

items.name: Sarai M.
items.text: Incredible experience!! The place is amazing 😍. 100% recommended.

items.name: Kathy B.
items.text: Beautiful monument!! This is a must see!!

items.name: Thomas G.
items.text: The location is stunning! Surrounded by nature and the river fed by the crystal-clear waters of the Blue Eye spring.

officialManagement.title: About Broken Chair
officialManagement.text: Broken Chair is a protected nature reserve in Albania, celebrated for its unique geological features and stunning beauty. The reserve is officially managed by the National Agency of Protected Areas (AKZM) and the Ministry of Tourism and Environment, dedicated to preserving the fragile karst hydrological ecosystem.
